import argparse
import json
import os

import pandas as pd

from model import load_checkpoint


def main():
    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
    ap.add_argument("--data_path", required=True)
    ap.add_argument("--checkpoint", required=True)
    ap.add_argument("--output", required=True)
    args = ap.parse_args()

    csv_path = os.path.join(args.data_path, "test.csv")
    if not os.path.exists(csv_path):
        # local smoke-test convenience only; official eval provides test.csv
        if os.path.exists(os.path.join(args.data_path, "val.csv")):
            csv_path = os.path.join(args.data_path, "val.csv")
        else:
            csv_path = os.path.join(args.data_path, "train.csv")
    df = pd.read_csv(csv_path)
    seqs = df["sequence"].astype(str).tolist()
    ckpt = load_checkpoint(args.checkpoint)
    model = ckpt["model"]
    preds = model.predict(seqs)
    preds = [float(x) for x in preds]
    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(args.output) or ".", exist_ok=True)
    with open(args.output, "w") as f:
        json.dump({"predictions": preds}, f)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
